title: Pilocereus millspaughii
intro: Pilocereus millspaughii, also known as the "Millspaugh's Pincushion Cactus," is a species of cactus in the genus Pilocereus that is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Pilocereus millspaughii include flavonoids, glycosides, and alkaloids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. In traditional herbal medicine, Pilocereus millspaughii is often used to prepare infusions, teas, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, fever, and inflammation.
taxonomy: Pilocereus millspaughii is classified within the family Cactaceae, which is the family of cacti plants. This plant belongs to the genus Pilocereus, which is a group of epiphytic cacti. Pilocereus millspaughii is further classified as Pilocereus millspaughii (Britton & Rose) Backeb., making it a specific species within the genus. The classification of Pilocereus millspaughii is based on its physical characteristics and genetic makeup. This classification helps to understand the evolutionary history and relationships of this species within the plant kingdom.

The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various conditions such as fever, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. side_effects: Pilocereus millspaughii contains a chemical called choline that can cause gastrointestinal upset, including nausea and vomiting, when ingested. In high doses, it may also lead to headaches, dizziness, and other central nervous system effects. The plant's sap can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals.
